agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 Annual Municipal Report presented to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to receive and consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a shorter Highlights version. This report summarizes the city's finances, services, and achievements for the past year. It is a proposed overview for council's information and discussion, not a final decision or new law. No immediate changes to business rules or fees are proposed in this item.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
2025 ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT AND ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT HIGHLIGHTS
Purpose: To present the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 proposed, effective July 1
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal the current Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replace it with a new Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The changes would also update the Burnaby Building Bylaw and the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, introducing new development permit types, expanded delegated authorities, and formalized fee and refund practices. The new bylaw has not been approved yet — it is up for a council decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new rules and fees for development and building permits will take effect July 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT OF BURNABY DEVELOPMENT PROCEDURES BYLAW AND RELATED AMENDMENTS TO THE BURNABY BUILDING BYLAW AND BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw, 2022
and replace it with the proposed
Development Procedures Bylaw, 2026
, to amend the
Burnaby
Building Bylaw, 2016
and
Burnaby
Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

attachments.title: Proposed Repeal and Replacement of Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw and Rela

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to apply for award on development approvals process
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing an application for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards. This is based on the final report for Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. The DAP program aims to make the process of getting approvals for development projects faster and more efficient. If endorsed, the application would be submitted to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) for a chance to win an award recognizing the program's success. This is a proposed step, not a final decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
DAP PROGRAM - PHASE 3 FINAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for mobile devices and plans
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, service plans, and data services for city staff. If approved, this contract would set the terms and costs for the city's mobile phone and data needs for a set period. This is a routine procurement decision and does not directly change any rules or fees for local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - MOBILE DEVICE AND PLANS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on aeration and overseeding contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. If approved, this would mean the city hires a company to aerate (poke small holes in the soil to let air, water, and nutrients reach the roots) and overseed (spread new grass seed over existing lawns) on city-owned land. This is a routine maintenance contract and does not create new rules or fees for businesses. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - AERATION AND OVERSEEDING SERVICES
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for aeration and overseeding services.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Victory St. road upgrades
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for road upgrades on Victory Street, between Patterson Avenue and Jubilee Avenue. If approved, this would fund construction work to improve the road. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been made.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - (CB-3233) VICTORY ST. - PATTERSON TO JUBILEE ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Victory St. – Patterson to Jubilee Road Upgrades project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on sidewalk contract for Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for a sidewalk package project on Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets. If approved, this would mean new sidewalks will be built on these streets. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - (CA-3299) TRIUMPH, CARLETON, ROSSER, GILMORE, AND MADISON SIDEWALK PACKAGE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison Sidewalk Package project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby's Sidewalk Expansion Program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for the Sidewalk Expansion Program (project EB-8022). If approved, this change would alter the terms of the city's contract to build or expand sidewalks. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - (EB-8022) SIDEWALK EXPANSION PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EB-8022) Sidewalk Expansion Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 amendments for Maple Ridge and Surrey
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the Metro 2050 regional growth plan. One change is proposed by the City of Maple Ridge (for land near North 256 Street) and the other by the City of Surrey (for the Hazelmere area). These are site-specific amendments that could affect how land is used and developed in those areas. Council has not yet decided what to say in the letters — this item is up for discussion and a vote at the meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
COMMENT LETTERS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENTS: CITY OF MAPLE RIDGE (NORTH 256 STREET) and SURREY (HAZELMERE)
Purpose:
To inform Council about two different site-specific Metro 2050 Amendments, one proposed by the City of Maple Ridge and the other by the City of Surrey, and to consider forwarding comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to review street width standards for new developments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council has directed city staff to review the current street width standards. This means the rules about how wide new streets must be in Burnaby could change. The review is just starting — no new rules have been passed yet. If the standards are changed, it could affect how much space is needed for roads in new housing or commercial projects. Business owners involved in construction, development, or property management should watch for updates, as narrower or wider streets could impact project costs and design.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on updated street width standards — this could affect your development or construction project costs and design.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - PROPOSED WORKS AND SERVICES REQUIREMENTS
Purpose: To direct staff to review the City's street width standards.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on consultant budget cuts for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal that would direct staff to prepare a detailed list of consultant budget cuts for 2026. The list would show which consultant contracts are being cut, which departments are affected, and which consultants would still get funding. This is not a final decision yet — council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting. If approved, staff would then bring back the actual list of cuts for another vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if you work with or rely on city consultants, this could affect future contracts.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - 2026 CONSULTANT FUNDNG AND BUDGET REDUCTIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for staff to provide a comprehensive list of consultant budget cuts, including associated functions and departments, and a separate list of consultants retaining funding for 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider an update to the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan. This proposed plan would set new rules for how trails on Burnaby Mountain are used, maintained, and managed. The plan is not yet approved — council will discuss it and decide whether to give staff direction to move forward. If adopted, the plan could affect how businesses and the public access and use trails in the area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect trail access for your business or events on Burnaby Mountain.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PRCC - DRAFT BURNABY MOUNTAIN TRAIL MANAGEMENT PLAN
Purpose: To provide an update and seek Council direction on the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider supporting Vancouver Roques Rugby's 2028 Bingham Cup bid
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a request from Vancouver Roques Rugby to support their bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028. The Bingham Cup is an international rugby tournament for gay and inclusive teams. Council will consider whether to officially back the bid, which could bring visitors and economic activity to the region. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - VANCOUVER ROQUES RUGBY - BINGHAM CUP 2028 SUPPORT REQUEST
Purpose: To seek Council support for Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Non-market housing project at Norland Avenue update
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will receive an update on a proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue. Non-market housing means homes that are not sold at regular market prices — they are kept affordable for lower-income households. This is an update only; no final decision or vote is scheduled at this meeting. The project is still in the proposal stage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
PROPOSED NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECT AT 3961 AND 3983 NORLAND AVENUE
Purpose: To provide an update on the proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on Burnaby civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a status update on significant civic projects at an upcoming meeting. This is a report-back item, not a final decision. The update will cover the progress of major city-building initiatives, such as new community centres, parks, or infrastructure. Business owners should watch for details on how these projects could affect local traffic, construction timelines, and nearby commercial activity. No vote on the projects themselves is scheduled at this time.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor the council meeting agenda for the full project list and any impacts on your business area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on major engineering projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a status update on major engineering projects covering December 2025 to April 2026. This is a report for information only — no decision or bylaw is being voted on at this time. The update will outline progress on key infrastructure work, but no new rules or fees are proposed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - DECEMBER 2025 - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on IT initiatives for early 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the city's IT initiatives for January to March 2026. This is a report to keep council informed about ongoing technology projects and their progress. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time, but the update may signal future decisions that could affect how businesses interact with the city online or through digital services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
FMC - SU: IT INITIATIVES - JANUARY TO MARCH 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the IT initiatives for January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby recreation study on inclusion and accessibility
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College. This study looks at how to make public recreation programs and spaces more welcoming and accessible for Burnaby’s diverse communities. The project is still in progress — no new rules or policies have been approved yet. Council is set to review the emerging insights and decide on next steps.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
PRCC - INCLUSION, DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND ACCESSIBILITY (IDEAS) IN PUBLIC RECREATION RESEARCH IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College, outlining its purpose, partnership model, and progress to date, while sharing emerging insights and demonstrating how the work supports improved access, inclusion, and participation in public recreation for Burnaby’s diverse communities.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Community Safety Plan progress update for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a progress report on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan and consider an update for 2026. This is a status update only — no new rules or bylaws are being voted on at this time. The plan covers local safety programs, crime prevention, and community well-being initiatives. Business owners should be aware that future changes to safety-related bylaws or programs could follow from this planning process.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
PSC - SU: BURNABY COMMUNITY SAFETY AND WELL-BEING PLAN PROGRESS REPORT AND 2026 UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive Burnaby Fire Department status update for February 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update from the Burnaby Fire Department covering operations up to the end of February 2026. This is a routine report to keep council informed about the department's activities, response times, and any emerging issues. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time — the update is for information only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.7
PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (FEBRUARY 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of February 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review RCMP performance and policing update for Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status report on RCMP policing operations for December 2025 to January 2026, along with the quarterly Annual Performance Plan (APP) report for October to December 2025. This is a routine update — no new rules or fees are proposed. The report gives Council a look at how local policing is going, including crime stats and response times. No decision is being made at this meeting; it is just for information.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.8
PSC - SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (DECEMBER 2025 - JANUARY 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (OCTOBER - DECEMBER 2025)
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering October to December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to replace 1965 rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed new zoning bylaw for Burnaby. A bylaw is a local law that controls how land and buildings can be used. The current rules date back to 1965. The proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 would repeal the old 1965 bylaw and replace it with updated rules, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines (rules for getting permits to build or develop) and new zoning district assignments (which areas are for homes, businesses, or industry). This is a first reading — the first step in the approval process. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak up about how the new rules could affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14815 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby zoning bylaw enforcement update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces its zoning rules. The amendment would replace the old 1965 Zoning Bylaw with the new 2026 Zoning Bylaw in the city's enforcement system. This means if the new bylaw passes, the city would use the updated 2026 rules when issuing tickets or fines for zoning violations. The change is about which set of rules the city can enforce — it does not change the zoning rules themselves at this stage.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, your property must follow the new 2026 Zoning Bylaw for enforcement purposes.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14816 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (repeal Schedule A, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 section and replace with Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend affordable housing definitions in fee waiver bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the definitions of 'affordable housing' and 'Zoning Bylaw' in the existing Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) Bylaw. Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges are fees the city collects from developers to help pay for community amenities like parks and infrastructure. If approved, this amendment could affect which housing projects qualify for fee reductions or waivers, potentially impacting development costs and housing affordability in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development project's eligibility for fee waivers.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14817 - Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of ACC and DCC Bylaw 2024 (definitions of "affordable housing" and "Zoning Bylaw")

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 for redevelopment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create new protections for tenants whose homes are being redeveloped. The 'Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026' aims to help renters who are displaced when their building is torn down or renovated. This is a first reading, meaning it is an early step in the approval process. The bylaw has not been passed yet, and council will consider it further before making a final decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to tell council your views on the proposed tenant protections.
agenda_briefs.industries: Tenant, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protection enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would update how the city enforces tenant protection rules. If approved, it would give the city new tools to deal with landlords who break tenant protection bylaws. The amendment is called 'Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026'. It is still a proposal — it has not been passed yet. Council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how tenant complaints are handled in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby mid-year fee changes up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed mid-year amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, this would update various fees the city charges for permits, licences, and services. The exact fee changes are not detailed in this agenda item, but the bylaw amendment would allow the city to adjust its fee schedule partway through the year. Business owners should watch for the council decision to see if fees affecting their operations will change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this bylaw amendment — it could change fees you pay for city permits or licences.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.6
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on final adoption of e-bike pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would make changes to the existing e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program. The amendment updates the Street and Traffic Bylaw to adjust rules for these vehicles. This is not yet law — Council will decide whether to give final approval at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new e-bike and scooter rules could affect your business operations or deliveries.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike pilot rules in parks up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw that would make the e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program permanent or extend it. This proposed bylaw change would update the rules for using e-bikes and electric kick scooters in city parks. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at the next council meeting. If passed, the amendment would change what types of electric bikes and scooters are allowed on park paths and where they can go.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could affect where you can ride e-bikes or scooters in Burnaby parks.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Parks e-bike pilot bylaw final adoption vote

agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the existing Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to support a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this change would update how the city enforces rules for these devices during the pilot. The amendment is up for final adoption at the next council meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposed change that council will decide on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new enforcement rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations.

#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets new property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for all taxable land and buildings (called 'improvements') in the city for the 2026 tax year. It is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which give the city the authority to set these rates. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you will owe next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your property tax bill for 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 final adoption vote

agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services

Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on removing office space for 292 more homes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw amendment for the Grand Promenade site (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place). The original plan, approved in September 2024, allowed two residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) plus a 13-storey podium with retail and office space. The new proposal would remove the commercial office use and replace it with 292 additional market strata residential units (condos). This change has not been approved yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — you can attend to voice support or concerns about this rezoning change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14737 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 and 73 storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant is proposing to remove commercial office use and replace it with additional market strata residential use, which includes the proposal of 292 additional market strata units
